title: Aureline canonical history-row, timeline-group, narrative-summary-card, and chronology-export-preview contract
description: Boundary schema for the four chronology primitives frozen in docs/ux/chronology_row_contract.md. The history_row_record is one structured event-or-history row keyed by history_row_id and bound to a canonical_object_target_ref; the timeline_group_record is one grouping rule over one or more history rows sharing a scope, object, or grouped-burst lineage; the narrative_summary_card_record is one human-readable summary card that cites its constituent history rows by id without inventing per-surface fields; the chronology_export_preview_record is one in-product preview of a chronology export (support bundle, AI-evidence packet, policy-audit export, publication proof) that MUST preserve the same structured vocabulary and object identity carried on the live surface. Adding a new action_verb, scope_object_kind, outcome_class, importance_class, provenance_badge_class, detail_link_kind, timeline_group_rule, export_preview_kind, or redaction_posture is additive-minor and requires a history_row_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row. Raw bodies, raw paths, raw URLs, raw prompt text, raw completion text, and raw credential material never cross the RPC boundary; this schema carries ids, typed vocabulary, and short privacy-safe summary labels only. This schema is the cross-tool chronology boundary every non-owning surface reads; the owning runtime's Rust types remain the schema of record.

history_row_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this chronology-primitive vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / AI-evidence packets / exported chronology previews. Raw bodies, raw paths, raw URLs, raw prompt text, and raw credential material never appear here.

instance_schema_hint.description: Optional schema hint carried on worked fixtures.

short_label_text.description: Short, privacy-safe label text intended for direct rendering on a chronology surface or export preview. MUST NOT embed raw paths, raw URLs, raw secret material, raw customer-owned identifiers, or raw AI prompt / completion text.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source. Wall-clock display text is a derived concern carried in display_time_policy; the canonical storage timestamp is always UTC and monotonic so that ordering, export, and replay remain reproducible across time-zone, daylight-saving, and skew boundaries (M00-26).

exercised_axes.description: Optional worked-example summary of the vocabulary values the fixture exercises.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional worked-example prelude carried on fixtures.

redaction_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011). The broker-owned redaction pass (ADR-0007) runs before bytes reach any persistent or exportable sink, including exported chronology previews and AI-evidence packets.

scope_object_kind.description: Closed vocabulary naming what kind of canonical object this row is about. A surface that invents a private scope_object_kind for one of these classes is non-conforming (denial_reason = scope_object_kind_aliased).

actor_kind.description: Closed actor-kind vocabulary. user_actor names a human operator of this product install; system_actor names background automation owned by the product itself; ai_agent_actor names an AI apply / suggestion path; remote_service_actor names a non-AI hosted service (sync mirror, symbol service, provider); admin_policy_actor names a policy authority acting on behalf of the deployment; extension_actor names an installed extension; unknown_actor is reserved for imported external audit trails whose actor identity is not resolvable at boundary time.

action_verb.description: Closed stable-verb vocabulary for history rows. Verbs name what happened to the canonical object on this row. acknowledge / dismiss / resolve distinctions from the notification-delivery contract survive verbatim: acknowledged removes the badge but not the row, dismissed removes the transient surface without mutating the row, succeeded / failed / cancelled record outcome. widened is reserved for rare rebaseline events where a narrowed grant, scope, or waiver is intentionally restored by an authority; a widening that lacks an accompanying policy-decision row is non-conforming (denial_reason = widening_without_policy_basis).

outcome_class.description: Closed outcome-class vocabulary. observed_only is reserved for rows that record a state observation without mutating the object (provider-sync health tick, policy-decision observation).

importance_class.description: Closed importance-class vocabulary. routine rows MAY be truncated inside a timeline_group or a narrative_summary_card when the group_rule so permits; consequential and safety_critical rows MUST expose a non-truncating detail_link with detail_link_kind in {canonical_object_target_exact, evidence_packet_row, review_sheet, diff_view, durable_job_row_exact, full_detail_sheet} so the user can reach the full truth of that row (denial_reason = high_importance_row_truncated_without_detail_link).

provenance_badge_class.description: Closed provenance-badge vocabulary. Every history row MUST name exactly one. first_party_direct_observation names a row recorded by a subsystem inside this product install from its own observation; first_party_synthesized_summary names a row derived by the product from other rows; ai_assisted names a row whose content is partly generated by an AI apply / suggestion path with human confirmation; ai_generated_summary names a narrative summary written entirely by AI — the row MUST preserve the underlying evidence rows by id and MUST NOT replace them; imported_from_external_audit_trail names a row re-published from an upstream audit stream whose raw body never crossed the RPC boundary; reconstructed_from_backup names a row reconstituted from a backup / support bundle whose original source is unavailable.

detail_link_kind.description: Closed detail-link vocabulary. A history row without a detail_link is non-conforming; a row that silently falls back to a generic home screen, a search result, or an external URL is non-conforming (denial_reason = detail_link_silently_downgraded). audit_trail_only is reserved for rows whose exact durable row is policy-blocked or redacted but a durable audit-trail entry still exists; not_available_linkback_lost is reserved for rows whose durable target has been permanently lost (moved / removed / failed recovery) and MUST carry a non-null unavailability_reason_label. full_detail_sheet is the chronology-side full-detail target that consequential and safety_critical rows open in place of a transient surface.

absolute_time_format_class.description: Closed absolute-time presentation vocabulary. The canonical storage timestamp is always monotonic_timestamp (ISO 8601 UTC); this axis names the presentation the surface is committing to for the absolute-time rendering on the row. Export previews MUST pin iso_8601_utc so the exported payload is independent of the exporter's local time zone (M00-26).

relative_time_format_class.description: Closed relative-time presentation vocabulary. not_rendered is permitted only when the surface explicitly suppresses relative time (e.g., signing_evidence_only redaction where skew labeling is required instead).

time_zone_class.description: Closed time-zone class vocabulary. utc pins UTC in both storage and display and is required for signing_evidence_only rows and exported chronology previews. device_local_iana renders in the device's IANA time zone. deployment_pinned_iana renders in an admin-pinned IANA zone. observer_skew_labeled is reserved for rows whose display time zone is derived from an observing client whose wall clock is unreliable; the label MUST say so.

display_time_zone_iana.description: IANA time zone name when time_zone_class is device_local_iana or deployment_pinned_iana. Null otherwise.

both_forms_coexist_on_row.description: True when the surface commits to rendering both absolute and relative time on the same row (hover, focus, or inline). Chronology surfaces MUST commit to true on consequential and safety_critical rows so a reader does not need to choose between 'when exactly' and 'how long ago'.

display_time_policy.description: Per-row presentation policy for time. Canonical storage is always monotonic_timestamp (ISO 8601 UTC); this object names the surface's committed display rendering and is carried through narrative summary cards and export previews without re-invention.

trust_state.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state at the moment the row was minted.

execution_context_id.description: ADR-0009 execution-context id when the row was minted inside a live execution. Null when the row was minted outside an execution context.

policy_context.description: Policy context the row was minted under. Re-validated on every render and on every export.

target_identity_ref.description: Opaque target id. Null only when kind is audit_trail_only, not_available_linkback_lost, or full_detail_sheet that is generated on demand from this row's payload.

is_durable.description: True when the target row persists across the current session. A detail_link on a consequential or safety_critical history row MUST be durable.

unavailability_reason_label.description: Required (non-null) when kind is not_available_linkback_lost or audit_trail_only. Explains why the exact row is not reachable (moved / missing extension / policy-blocked / stale remote / failed recovery).

announcement_label.description: Short accessibility-announcement label read when the detail link receives focus.

detail_link_record.description: One detail-link target on a history row. Every row carries exactly one; generic home-screen, search, or external-URL fallbacks are forbidden.

actor_identity_ref.description: Opaque actor id. Null only when actor_kind is system_actor or unknown_actor. A user_actor, ai_agent_actor, remote_service_actor, admin_policy_actor, or extension_actor MUST carry a non-null ref.

linked_canonical_event_id_ref.description: Optional back-reference to a canonical_event_id carried on the event-lineage record (schemas/ux/event_lineage.schema.json) when this history row shares lineage with a notification-delivery trail. Preserves the same id across the live surface, the history lane, and any export preview.

linked_evidence_packet_id_ref.description: Optional back-reference to an evidence-packet id on ai_evidence_row, publication_row, or support_bundle_row.

linked_task_event_id_ref.description: Optional back-reference to a task_event_envelope_record id on task_run_row.

linked_recovery_snapshot_id_ref.description: Optional back-reference to a recovery snapshot id on recovery_snapshot_row.

supersedes_history_row_id_ref.description: Required (non-null) when action_verb = superseded. Names the previous history row this row replaces — the prior row MUST still be reachable from the chronology and MUST NOT be erased.

allOf.description: actor_kind in {user_actor, ai_agent_actor, remote_service_actor, admin_policy_actor, extension_actor} MUST carry actor_identity_ref.
importance_class in {consequential, safety_critical} MUST carry a detail_link whose kind is a non-truncating durable target.
redaction_class = signing_evidence_only MUST use time_zone_class = utc and absolute_time_format_class = iso_8601_utc.

history_row_record.description: One structured event/history row. One row per (canonical_object_target_ref, action_verb, monotonic_timestamp) triple; a surface that mints two rows with the same triple but different ids is non-conforming (denial_reason = duplicate_history_row_for_canonical_action).

timeline_group_rule.description: Closed grouping-rule vocabulary. explicit_user_pin names a user-authored grouping and is the only rule allowed to mix scope_object_kinds freely.

canonical_object_target_ref.description: Required (non-null) when group_rule is same_canonical_object. Null otherwise.

grouped_burst_id_ref.description: Required (non-null) when group_rule is same_grouped_burst_id. Null otherwise. Carries the grouped_burst_id minted on the event-lineage record.

linked_canonical_event_id_ref.description: Required (non-null) when group_rule is same_linked_canonical_event_id. Null otherwise.

member_history_row_ids.description: Opaque references to the history_row_record ids that belong to this group. Grouping never erases a row; a member row stays reachable by id from the chronology even when the group header is collapsed.

allow_routine_row_truncation.description: True only when importance_class is routine AND every member row carries importance_class = routine. A group containing any consequential or safety_critical member MUST set false; setting true on such a group is non-conforming (denial_reason = group_truncates_consequential_member).

timeline_group_record.description: One timeline-group rule over one or more history rows. Groups are structural overlays, not replacements; member rows remain canonically reachable by id.

cited_history_row_ids.description: Opaque references to the history_row_record ids cited by this card. A narrative card that cites no rows is non-conforming (denial_reason = narrative_card_without_cited_rows). An ai_generated_summary MUST cite every row whose content it summarized.

ai_confidence_class.description: Required (non-null) when provenance_badge_class is ai_generated_summary or ai_assisted. Exposes confidence honestly; a card without disclosure is non-conforming (denial_reason = ai_narrative_without_confidence_disclosure).

allOf.description: provenance_badge_class = ai_generated_summary MUST carry ai_confidence_class != not_applicable_non_ai_summary.
importance_class in {consequential, safety_critical} MUST carry a non-truncating durable detail_link.

narrative_summary_card_record.description: One narrative summary card over one or more history rows. A summary card never replaces its cited rows; a reader can always reach the underlying rows by id from the chronology.

export_preview_kind.description: Closed export-preview vocabulary. Every preview carries exactly one kind.

export_redaction_posture.description: Closed export-redaction posture. An export preview MAY narrow redaction (operator_only, internal_support_only, signing_evidence_only) relative to the live surface; it MUST NOT widen it. An export whose posture widens live-surface redaction is non-conforming (denial_reason = export_widened_redaction_relative_to_live_surface).

included_history_row_ids.description: Opaque references to the history_row_record ids included in this preview. Object identity and structured vocabulary on every included row MUST be identical to the live-surface row (same canonical_object_target_ref, same action_verb, same outcome_class, same importance_class, same provenance_badge_class, same scope_object_kind); a preview that renames or replaces any of these is non-conforming (denial_reason = export_preview_field_renamed_from_live_surface).

excluded_row_count_by_reason.description: Structured accounting for rows present on the live surface that are excluded from this preview. A preview MUST disclose the reason counts rather than silently dropping rows (denial_reason = export_preview_silently_dropped_rows).

allOf.description: An export preview MUST pin display_time_policy.time_zone_class = utc and absolute_time_format_class = iso_8601_utc so exported payloads are independent of the exporter's local time zone.
export_redaction_posture = preserve_live_surface_redaction requires effective_redaction_class to equal the live-surface class of the subject.

chronology_export_preview_record.description: One in-product chronology-export preview. A preview MUST preserve object identity and structured vocabulary identical to the live surface; the only permitted differences are narrower redaction, a pinned UTC time zone, and the explicit excluded_row_count_by_reason accounting.
